behavior_summary: This script provides a collection of small utility routines that present static book/log text to the player using Book::read and wait for the player to finish reading.

U8P_BOOKMARK::func0080 is the primary entry: it launches a Book::read for the 'KILLER JOKES' text, suspends until the read process ends, spawns a short-lived free process (FREE::func1C19) with a literal argument, suspends again for that spawn-related timing, then calls METHOD::func0676 (likely a cleanup or post-read handler).

All other functions (U8P_BOOKMARK::func01EB, ::func0700, ::func0C03, ::func12CC, ::func175F, ::func1E2E, ::func294B, ::func310D, ::func394D, ::func4357, ::func443C, ::func456C, ::func4678) are simple wrappers that call Book::read with different hard-coded text strings and suspend until completion.
notable_control_flow: U8P_BOOKMARK::func0080: Book::read(...) -> capture pid -> suspend (wait for reader) -> spawn FREE::func1C19(0x00000000) -> suspend -> METHOD::func0676()
Each helper function: Book::read(literal_string, this) -> capture pid/temp -> suspend (wait for reader).
No global flags are read or written by any function in this file; behavior is UI/display only.
open_questions: What is the specific purpose of FREE::func1C19 spawned by U8P_BOOKMARK::func0080 (timing/animation/cleanup)? (confidence: low — inferred only from spawn pattern)
What does METHOD::func0676 do after the read/spawn sequence (cleanup, state update, or an object-specific follow-up)? (confidence: low — name and ordering suggest post-read handling)

Under which in-game event(s) are these functions invoked (direct object 'read' use, context menus, or other triggers)? The metadata suggests object-use/read but the exact hookup is not present. (confidence: medium)

main_function.name: U8P_BOOKMARK::func0080
main_function.purpose: Show the 'KILLER JOKES' book text to the player, wait until reading finishes, run a short-lived auxiliary spawn, wait/allow its timing, then invoke a follow-up method (likely cleanup/post-read handling).
entry_conditions: Invoked when the player interacts with/reads the bookmark/book object (appears to be triggered by a read/use action on the object).
Likely called by the engine when the object receives a 'use/read' event (inferred from Book::read usage and process-suspend pattern).
